title: Evangelions Narrative Annoisms WARNING HUGE PICTURES
display_title: Evangelions Narrative Annoisms WARNING HUGE PICTURES
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion's Narrative, Annoisms (WARNING: HUGE PICTURES)             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jan 21, 2009 1:41 pm This thread is about narrative and narrative tools utilized in Evangelion. Reasons for existence of this thread One: Since Jimbo has been gone (and shall for some time still) there haven't been much talk about Eva's narrative qualities and elements. Two: As I've been watching a lot of Kare Kano and Shiki Jitsu lately I've come to respect Anno even more as a director and he has become absolutely one of my all time favourites even if forgetting Eva. I had quite a revelation when I noticed that traffic lights that Anno uses in his pillow shots in (esp.) early episodes of Kare Kano reflect and symbolize moods of characters and/or plotpoints. That's way too fucking clever and subtle for frickin' comedy show, geez. So I decided to check out if I could decipher some fantastic little quirks and genius touches that elevates even more mundane scenes to higher levels in his works. This led me to rewatching many, many scenes and eps of Eva, especially early ones in search for all kind of subtle crap I might've missed. It wasn't for nothing. I had never noticed fisheye lens were used in Ep 12... I guess these things are interesting if you're interested in directing and later on (not on this first post) I'm going to discus few things I have suspicions on being deliberate "Annoisms" no one has ever mentioned before. Motifs etc. are old stuff to veterans of this forum, but of course I mention them too... From what I've gathered these are defining traits and obvious indicators of Anno's pure skill and brilliance as director we see in NGE: - his highly effective usage of offbeat camera angles (e.g camera looking up from floor in ep 2 elevator scene) -framing (just check out how bloody many times Anno balances characters on opposite ends of screen and puts some object between them, or how framing is used in Shiki Jitsu to give feeling of entrapment etc.) - camerawork (e.g Shinji strangling Asuka scene in EoE is absolutely brilliant in this department. (It is of course brilliant in every way possible)) - in true Japanese fashion: pillow shots (they're everywhere and I'd blame NGE for making them far more common in anime than they were before series came out) - rhyming and pacing of scenes and events (take for example scene in ep 23 where Ritsuko stares picture of her, Gendo and her mother from computer and start to count each time shot changes. Or hell, you could do that with any scene featuring clear rhyming) - brilliant editing (Anno utilizes to maximum effect the close-closer-FAR OUT camera in scenes such as where Shinji first "meets" GNR ((this belongs under camerawork category too, these overlap a lot)), and of course the ever working classic having long shots that explode to quick cuts) - use of contrasting elements and contrapunct (most obvious example: end of the world set to Hey Jude pastiche. It's also best example) - motifs (in NGE obvious ones like the waterdrop and Shinji's hand...) - lots of echoing (large part of "you never see anything once" goes under this category instead of "lol saving budget". Apart from bridge bunnies material of course) - brilliant use of repetition (happens A LOT in NGE, e.g Asuka and bath scene in Ep 22) - Godardesque imagerape montages - "Flash" images (scene might be interrupted suddenly by shot that last just second or so, being barely noticeable) - Fish eye lens (now that's effect that's been horribly raped by lots of awful indie films, but Anno is masterful with that effect. For example see "Hell Train" sequences.) - Colours Red and combinations of Red/Blue (Anno has fetish for red anyway, and blue shows up a lot in relation with it. For example of latter see 75 % of Shiki Jitsu, "kiss scene rebuild" in EoE, Ep 8 of Kare Kano... As for red being everywhere: Just watch anything starting from Gunbuster, minus Nadia) - Usage of titles as storytelling device (well, obviously whole of last two eps) - short "dead" shots that represent whole "failure of communication" theme in Evangelion and/or is used to throw of narrative flow to nerve viewers (kind of hard to explain, they're obvious when viewing. If you notice then consciously that is, they're easy to miss. Example of former usage; for example after the long "nothing happeining" shot of Rei and Asuka in elevator. Asuka starts to rage and when Rei answers the shot going with dialogue is usually of her back or Asuka staring her back. There's point in which Asuka asks something and clearly waits for answer; we get shot of Asuka looking expectantly Rei's back that lasts barely over second. There's no answer. Similarly there's quick cut to Shinji between exchange between him and Gendo in ep 19 when Shinji is "leaving NERV forever" - Shinji doesn't answer. Example of latter could be Shinji shouting of to his other me in Ep 16's mindfuck sequence. Instead of answer what he gets is very short, creepy close shot of shadowy little Shinji. When shot changes he finally answers... As I said this one's hard to explain without use of videos) Screenshot examples: Odd (but absolutely great, esp. in context and purpose it's used in) camera angle: [URL=http://img129.imageshack.us/my.php?image=ngeannofloorcameraie2.png] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img129/ngeannofloorcameraie2.png/1/] [/URL] Framing and objectz on front: [URL=http://img218.imageshack.us/my.php?image=ngeannocameraangleobjeceg8.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img218/ngeannocameraangleobjeceg8.jpg/1/] [/URL] [URL=http://img218.imageshack.us/my.php?image=shikijitsuframinggd7.png] [/URL] Camerawork: Well obviously I can't do this because I'm too lazy to make GIFs. Rest: they'll come once imageshack stops sucking at uploading

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jan 21, 2009 2:00 pm Camerawork, editing and framing all exemplified by this: [URL=http://img141.imageshack.us/my.php?image=lilithshinjiru1.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img141/lilithshinjiru1.jpg/1/] [/URL] See how the brilliantly build tension by "closing in" on two "elements" of scene (Lilith and Shinji's freaked out eye) are resolved by Major Chord Of Taking Big Step Away. (I should've become musicologist ) Godardesque mindrape flashing OH MY GOD I HAVE EPILEPGNYAAAAGGHH [URL=http://img132.imageshack.us/my.php?image=imagerapzr5.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img132/imagerapzr5.jpg/1/] [/URL] Fisheye lens: Used for subtle effect of making the doors more forebearing and "menacing": [URL=http://img132.imageshack.us/my.php?image=ngefisheyelensac0.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img132/ngefisheyelensac0.jpg/1/] [/URL] And in here to make very beautiful shot and give extra creepiness and otherworldiness to scene: [URL=http://img232.imageshack.us/my.php?image=ep16mindfuckfisheyelenshq6.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img232/ep16mindfuckfisheyelenshq6.jpg/1/] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jan 21, 2009 2:22 pm brilliant pacing and rhyming: [URL=http://img218.imageshack.us/my.php?image=ep15ax9.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img218/ep15ax9.jpg/1/] [/URL] (last pic got somewhat cut out but not like it matters...) Non-NGE (Gunbuster) example of Redbluism (because it is better example than anything out of NGE apart from certain EoE scene and I won't be taking that many screenshots!!!): [URL=http://img253.imageshack.us/my.php?image=gunbusterredbluecolourinc5.png] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img253/gunbusterredbluecolourinc5.png/1/] [/URL] Motif: [URL=http://img216.imageshack.us/my.php?image=ngedropub0.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img216/ngedropub0.jpg/1/] [/URL] Great (and obvious) example of Echoism AND parallelism: [URL=http://img88.imageshack.us/my.php?image=echoingparallelismzj7.jpg] [/URL] Pillowshots (used in here to set the place, and above all its mood; after all you can set place with one simple shot as most western moveis tend to do) example: [URL=http://img160.imageshack.us/my.php?image=pillowshotsuy8.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img160/pillowshotsuy8.jpg/1/] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jan 21, 2009 3:18 pm and the Repetition Trick [URL=http://img84.imageshack.us/my.php?image=ep22sl8.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://g.imageshack.us/img84/ep22sl8.jpg/1/] [/URL] (you have to save the pic and zoom it, for some reason imageshack leaves it that small in internet) whew... I guess this is my first major topic

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Jan 21, 2009 3:48 pm I just wish the quality of the writing could match that.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Jan 21, 2009 4:05 pm 56K warning much?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jan 21, 2009 5:00 pm I think I should clarify purpose of this thread: This is ment to be big, epochal thread about NGE's storytelling and narrative. It's faults too. There's lots of all kind of talk back from ANF era that are obsessed about narrative details and implications, resulting in sometimes great results and sometimes in hilarious fanwanking. I started the thread with directing and visual aspects, but this is "free word" topic foremost. It's ment to be as much about writing itself as the peculiar devices in which these writings are "realized". You can write about purpose of Penpen in early and late series just as well as obsess over cinematic details. BobBQ wrote: I just wish the quality of the writing could match that.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 21, 2009 6:43 pm Actually, the fish-eye lens example of the 'menacing gates' can also be interpreted as the world revolving solely around Asuka. The content of the shot is her not believing anything until she tries it herself, but the whole episode shows her as a literal 'warping' effect on the previous Shinji/Rei dynamic. Notice that throughout that episode she is constantly putting herself between them or otherwise separating Shinji from Rei. Even her choice of tactics at the end requires Rei to go away while she stays at the top with Shinji, fulfilling the same 'human shield' function Rei did against Ramiel.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 22, 2009 1:13 pm Xard wrote: BobBQ wrote: I just wish the quality of the writing could match that.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Thu Jan 22, 2009 2:38 pm I totally didn't notice the fisheye lens use in that scene with the doors! NeMZ wrote: Actually, the fish-eye lens example of the 'menacing gates' can also be interpreted as the world revolving solely around Asuka. The content of the shot is her not believing anything until she tries it herself, but the whole episode shows her as a literal 'warping' effect on the previous Shinji/Rei dynamic. Notice that throughout that episode she is constantly putting herself between them or otherwise separating Shinji from Rei. Even her choice of tactics at the end requires Rei to go away while she stays at the top with Shinji, fulfilling the same 'human shield' function Rei did against Ramiel.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jan 26, 2009 8:20 pm Not sure what else to say besides awesome thread. enkiv2: How can Asuka have the ability to wrap reality when she's just a regular kid like the other pilots (with the exception of Rei)?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Jan 27, 2009 12:54 am Asuka warps reality with her awesomeness, and, coupled with Shinji, it is warped even more by the WAFF.

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Tue Jan 27, 2009 9:53 am I wrote a very short paper on evangelion's cinematography for university and one of the things I picked up onand liked a lot is the show's quite exstensive use of close up's on the eyes of characters as demonstarted in some of the screen shots above. I thought it quite appropriate considering how they say the eyes are the windows of the soul but I also thought it very appropriate in that the show does focus on the characters thougths and emotions and the eyes are an excellent way of defining these. I also liked the drained or grainy colours that are used for the hospital and 'hell train' scenes. Because they are drained they emphasise the lonelyness of the situation the characters are in. There is a predominant use of Red and white throughout EoE in particular which aside from matching the colours of Rei's and Asuka's plugsuits is farily clever in that the Red's and oranges frame the hellish and stifling atmoshpere of the show whilst again the white represents purity and lonelyness at the same time. Then again maybe anno just had a fondness of these colours in particular. Dean

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Tue Jan 27, 2009 11:56 am This was one of the reasons I loved working with Jimbo on our short lived audio commentary project. Episode 1 is filled with these shots. -misato's face reflected in the elevator doors is replaced by Ritsuko's face as the doors open -Unit01's horn leading directly up to Gendou in the cage scene -Shinji bracketed by Misato and Ritsuko on the umbilical bridge.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Jan 27, 2009 12:58 pm Enki v.2 wrote: I totally didn't notice the fisheye lens use in that scene with the doors!

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Jan 27, 2009 1:10 pm I see.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Tue Jan 27, 2009 2:40 pm I see.

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Tue Jan 27, 2009 5:03 pm Red and white in EoE? Apart from Lilith& Red Earth and souls I don't see it. Unless you count Asuka & MPE's in it. As for Anno's intentions, I'm currently arguing about whether Anno had any grander purpose for Red/Blue colour schema or not. And as for the eyes shots; I'd never tought about it, I mean, aren't eye shots quite common in anime? But Eva does use them a lot...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jan 27, 2009 5:31 pm It's not just random. Bright red is a very strong color, giving high contrast against most neutrals like white or black. White and red also have cultural connotations of love (Japan's dual celebration of Valentine's Day and White Day) and sex (traditional red beans and rice to celebrate a Japanese girl's first period).

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Tue Jan 27, 2009 6:12 pm It's not just random. Bright red is a very strong color, giving high contrast against most neutrals like white or black. White and red also have cultural connotations of love (Japan's dual celebration of Valentine's Day and White Day) and sex (traditional red beans and rice to celebrate a Japanese girl's first period).

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Tue Jan 27, 2009 9:53 am I wrote a very short paper on evangelion's cinematography for university and one of the things I picked up onand liked a lot is the show's quite exstensive use of close up's on the eyes of characters as demonstarted in some of the screen shots above. I thought it quite appropriate considering how they say the eyes are the windows of the soul but I also thought it very appropriate in that the show does focus on the characters thougths and emotions and the eyes are an excellent way of defining these. I also liked the drained or grainy colours that are used for the hospital and 'hell train' scenes. Because they are drained they emphasise the lonelyness of the situation the characters are in. There is a predominant use of Red and white throughout EoE in particular which aside from matching the colours of Rei's and Asuka's plugsuits is farily clever in that the Red's and oranges frame the hellish and stifling atmoshpere of the show whilst again the white represents purity and lonelyness at the same time. Then again maybe anno just had a fondness of these colours in particular. Dean

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jan 27, 2009 5:31 pm It's not just random. Bright red is a very strong color, giving high contrast against most neutrals like white or black. White and red also have cultural connotations of love (Japan's dual celebration of Valentine's Day and White Day) and sex (traditional red beans and rice to celebrate a Japanese girl's first period).
